headline: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Head for Record as Chip Stocks Rally Rages On
summary: Stocks looked set to extend their record-breaking run on Wednesday after chip maker Advanced Micro Devices posted strong first-quarter earnings and President Donald Trump signaled there had been progress in peace talks between the U.S. and Iran.  S&P 500 futures added 0.3%, and contracts tied to the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 jumped 0.7%.  The S&P 500 and Nasdaq both closed at all-time highs on Tuesday as chip stocks racked up sizable gains, and AMD’s rock-solid results could extend the rally.

headline: S&P 500 and Nasdaq notch records; AMD results spark AI stock rally
summary:
STORY: U.S. stocks rallied on Wednesday, with the Dow climbing one-and-a-quarter percent, the S&P 500 adding nearly one-and-a-half percent and the Nasdaq soaring 2%.

Both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq notched more record high closes amid signs of a resolution in the Middle East conflict.

Wall Street was further buoyed by what Regan Capital founder Skyler Weinand called blockbuster earnings results, with S&P 500 companies on track for their strongest profit growth in more than four years.

"I think we're at 85% beat so far in first-quarter earnings, which is the highest I've ever seen, really. You have top-line and bottom-line growth, along with margin growth. Really, everything going on all cylinders here in the economy, leading for stocks to be at all time highs again.”

:: AMD

Shares of chipmakers continued their sharp climb, with Advanced Micro Devices surging almost 19% to an all-time high after forecasting quarterly revenue above expectations on robust demand for its data center chips.

Shares of rival Intel gained 4.5%, while the Philadelphia semiconductor index also rallied 4.5%, taking its gain so far this year to 62%.

:: Archive

Corning shares climbed 12% after the company said it was partnering with Nvidia to expand U.S. production of optical connectivity products used in AI data centers. Nvidia added more than 5.5%.

:: Archive

Shares of AI data center developer Hut 8 skyrocketed 35% after the company signed a 15-year lease worth $9.8 billion for its Beacon Point data center campus in Texas.

And shares of AI server maker Super Micro Computer rallied more than 24% following a stronger-than-expected forecast for fourth-quarter revenue and adjusted profit.

Elsewhere in the market, shares of Uber gained 8.5% after the ride-hailing and delivery platform forecast strong second-quarter bookings. 

Uber also said its growing adoption of - what else? - AI tools was helping to improve productivity across its operations.

headline: 'Insane' surge in chip stocks will help boost entire economy, CIO says
summary:
STORY: Advanced Micro Devices' shares jumped to an all-time high on Wednesday as its strong outlook boosted investor confidence about sustained demand for AI infrastructure, sparking a rally across global semiconductor stocks.

"Whether it's AMD, Nvidia, Intel, you name it, Taiwan Semiconductor, across the board, all of these names have tons of demand," Weinand said.

And with first-quarter earnings as a whole showing "margin growth, bottom line growth [and top line growth, I don't know where this train's really going to end," he added.
